name: Robin Kelly
bio: Robin Lynne Kelly was born April 30, 1956 in Harlem, New York, the daughter of a grocer. She moved to Illinois to attend Bradley University in Peoria, where she earned a B.A. in psychology (1977) and an M.A. in counseling (1982), and directed a crisis nursery while there. She later earned a Ph.D. in political science from Northern Illinois University in 2004. From 1992–2006 she served as Director of Community Affairs for Matteson, Illinois. She served in the Illinois House of Representatives (38th District) from 2003–2007, then became Chief of Staff to Illinois Treasurer Alexi Giannoulias — the first African American woman to serve as chief of staff to a statewide elected officeholder. She was appointed Cook County President Toni Preckwinkle's Chief Administrative Officer in 2011. Kelly won the 2013 special election to succeed Jesse Jackson Jr. in IL-02, and has been reelected continuously. She serves as Chair of the House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force. She announced her Senate candidacy on May 6, 2025, upon Dick Durbin's retirement. She chairs no major Senate committee but sits on the House Energy & Commerce Committee. Running a distant third in most polls of the Democratic primary, she has cited her cross-district legislative record and gun violence prevention work. (Sources: Wikipedia; robinkelly.house.gov; Capitol News Illinois; FEC S6IL00474)
prior_office: U.S. Representative (IL-02, 2013–present); Chair, Illinois Democratic Party (2021–2022); Cook County Chief Administrative Officer (2011–2013); Chief of Staff, IL State Treasurer (2007–2011); Illinois State Representative (38th District, 2003–2007)

jacket.note: FEC total raised for Senate committee S6IL00474 as of Q4 2025. Kelly raised $565,775 in Q2 2025 (April–June) and held ~$2M in her accounts as of mid-2025 per Capitol News Illinois (July 28, 2025). Trailing Krishnamoorthi ($30.5M) and Stratton ($4M+) substantially.

donors.name: Transfer from prior Robin Kelly House committee (H0IL02052)
donors.amount_note: FEC confirmed $2,152,081 transferred from prior House committee — largest single funding source for Senate bid

donors.name: Other Political Committee contributions (PAC aggregate)
donors.amount_note: FEC aggregate 'other_political_committee_contributions' — combined PAC receipts; individual PAC breakdown requires itemized FEC lookup

donors.name: American Mobilization PAC (Washington DC)
donors.amount_note: Largest single Q4 2025 donation per FEC data via The Telegraph

donors.name: Congressional Black Caucus PAC (CBCPAC)
donors.amount_note: CBC PAC listed as official endorser on Kelly campaign website; exact dollar amount pending itemized FEC lookup

donors.name: Corporate PACs (aggregate — multiple)
donors.amount_note: Capitol News Illinois confirmed Kelly, unlike Stratton, has accepted corporate PAC money; exact aggregate pending FEC itemized lookup

donors.name: Protect Progress PAC (Fairshake-aligned, pro-crypto)
donors.amount_note: Outside independent expenditure PAC — $89,900 spent supporting Kelly per Chicago Sun-Times (March 10, 2026). NOT direct campaign funds — cannot coordinate with campaign. Fairshake is funded primarily by Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz (gave $11M to MAGA Inc).

jacket.donors_note: Kelly is the lowest-funded of the three major candidates. Notable: Protect Progress (Fairshake-aligned, crypto PAC funded by Trump megadonors Andreessen/Horowitz) spent $89,900 in outside support for Kelly — political insiders say this is a Krishnamoorthi strategy to split the vote between Kelly and Stratton. Kelly herself said at a WTTW debate: 'super PAC money, dark money, to my knowledge, I haven't received any of that' — technically true of her campaign committee. (Source: Chicago Sun-Times, March 10, 2026)

red_flags.label: Forced Out as Illinois Democratic Party Chair — Pritzker Conflict
red_flags.detail: Kelly was elected Illinois Democratic Party Chair in March 2021, defeating a Pritzker-backed candidate (Chicago Ald. Michelle Harris). In July 2022, Kelly ended her bid for reelection as chair after it became clear she lacked support to beat State Rep. Lisa Hernandez — who was also backed by Gov. Pritzker. Capitol News Illinois reported the ouster as a Pritzker-engineered defeat. This conflict colors the current Senate race, where Pritzker is backing Stratton over Kelly. The Congressional Black Caucus publicly accused Pritzker of 'heavy-handing' the race. This is a pattern of Pritzker-vs.-Kelly conflict, now in its third round.

red_flags.label: Outside Support from Crypto PAC Linked to Trump Megadonors
red_flags.detail: Protect Progress, a PAC aligned with the Fairshake crypto PAC, spent $89,900 in outside support for Kelly. Fairshake is primarily funded by Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz (Andreessen Horowitz / a16z), who gave $11M to MAGA Inc. (a Trump-supporting PAC) per the New York Times. Political insiders believe the spending on Kelly is designed to split the Black vote with Stratton to benefit Krishnamoorthi. Kelly's campaign cannot legally coordinate with these PACs and has distanced itself from their support.

red_flags.label: Voted Yes on $26.4B Military Aid to Israel (2024)
red_flags.detail: Kelly voted yes on H.R. 8034 (April 20, 2024) providing $26.4B in emergency military aid to Israel and yes on H.R. 8369 (May 15, 2024) to release withheld weapons to Israel. Both are on the record. She also voted yes on the Yemen War Powers Resolution (2023) — an anti-war vote showing some limit to her hawkishness. Her voting record on Gaza/Israel is a mixed signal for progressive primary voters.

key_votes.bill: H.R. 8034 — Israel Security Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2024
key_votes.summary: Voted yes on $26.4B emergency military aid to Israel. Passed 366-58 on April 20, 2024.

key_votes.bill: H.R. 8369 — Israel Security Assistance Support Act, 2024
key_votes.summary: Voted yes to release withheld weapons to Israel. Passed 334-79 on May 15, 2024.

key_votes.bill: H.Con.Res. 21 — Remove US Forces from Yemen (War Powers Resolution), 2023
key_votes.summary: Voted yes to direct removal of US military from Yemen operations not authorized by Congress. Failed 102-321 on April 27, 2023.
Voted yes on legislation to ban semiautomatic assault weapons and high-capacity magazines. Passed 217-213 in the House. Did not advance in the Senate.
Voted yes on legislation investing $369B in climate/energy, extending ACA subsidies, and allowing Medicare drug price negotiation. Passed 220-207.
Kelly was primary sponsor. Reauthorizes research programs to reduce premature births and infant mortality. Enacted 2025.

key_votes.bill: H.R. 4387 — Maternal Health Quality Improvement Act of 2021
key_votes.summary: Kelly sponsored legislation addressing maternal mortality crisis, particularly for Black women. Enacted 2022.
Voted nay on legislation requiring detention of undocumented immigrants arrested for certain crimes. Passed 341-79 on January 14, 2026.

career_history.highlight: Chair, House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force; House Energy & Commerce Committee; succeeded Jesse Jackson Jr. via 2013 special election
Defeated Pritzker-backed candidate to win chair in 2021; did not seek reelection in 2022 after Pritzker again backed her opponent (Lisa Hernandez)
First African American woman to serve as chief of staff to an elected IL statewide officeholder
Served on Appropriations-Human Services, Mass Transit (Vice Chair), Housing & Urban Development committees

policy_platform.position: Lead champion in Congress for gun control legislation; authored bills on background check reform, assault weapon restrictions; chairs House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force since founding. Campaign platform calls her signature issue 'People Over Profits' and includes universal background checks and assault weapon bans.

policy_platform.topic: Healthcare — Medicare for All / Health Equity
policy_platform.position: Campaign website lists Medicare for All as a goal. Led legislation on maternal mortality (Maternal Health Quality Improvement Act, 2022) and premature births (PREEMIE Reauthorization Act, 2025). Focuses on health equity for Black and underserved communities.

Campaign platform 'People Over Profits' calls for minimum wage of $17/hour, minimum tax on individuals earning $100M+/year, union rights protections, and infrastructure investment in south suburban and rural IL-02 communities.
Supported federal decriminalization of marijuana (H.R. 3617, MORE Act, 2022). Supports American Dream and Promise Act for DACA/TPS recipients. Voted no on Laken Riley Act (2026).

social_pulse.summary: Positive among gun control advocates and Black political establishment. Supportive coverage from CAIR/Muslim civic groups notable given Gaza votes. Low social media footprint relative to Krishnamoorthi and Stratton — media portrays her as distant third in race. CBC endorsement triggered news cycle about Pritzker's 'heavy-handing.' Fairshake PAC support for Kelly drew negative attention and allegations she's being used as a vote-splitter.

trust_indicators.label: No personal ethics violations on record
Consistent gun control legislative record (12+ years)
Endorsed by Everytown for Gun Safety / Brady PAC
Endorsed by CAIR Midwest and Muslim civic orgs despite Israel votes
Voted YES — $26.4B military aid to Israel (April 2024)
Outside support from Fairshake-aligned crypto PAC (Protect Progress, $89.9K) — Trump megadonor funded
Ousted as IL Dem Party Chair by Pritzker-backed candidate (2022)

news_hits.headline: As Senate race tightens, Kelly benefits from super PAC war between Krishnamoorthi, Stratton
news_hits.summary: Chicago Sun-Times documents that Protect Progress PAC — aligned with Fairshake and funded by Trump megadonors Andreessen/Horowitz — spent $89,900 in outside support for Kelly, widely seen by political insiders as a Krishnamoorthi strategy to split the Black vote with Stratton. Kelly's own final TV ad shows opponents' attack ads and declares 'Oh hell no.'

news_hits.headline: Will Illinois' Democratic Primary for Senate Divide Black Voters?
news_hits.summary: The New York Times examines how both Stratton and Kelly are competing for the same Black voting base in a race where their combined support could hand a plurality to Krishnamoorthi. The Congressional Black Caucus's public call-out of Gov. Pritzker for 'heavy-handing' the race is highlighted.

news_hits.headline: AIPAC Is Staying Out of Illinois Senate Race — But Its Donors Back Juliana Stratton
news_hits.summary: The Intercept's reporting on AIPAC donor networks notes Kelly has received backing from CAIR Midwest and the Illinois Muslim Civic Coalition despite her yes votes on $26.4B Israel military aid — suggesting she is threading a difficult needle with progressive Muslim voters in the primary.

news_hits.headline: A historic Senate opening meets a divided Illinois Democratic Party
news_hits.title: A historic Senate opening meets a divided Illinois Democratic Party
news_hits.summary: Politico (March 12) analysis of the three-way Senate primary. Notes rival bids by Kelly and Stratton risk splitting support among Black voters as Krishnamoorthi leads early. Kelly is described as trailing well behind in polls.

news_hits.headline: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive primary preview (March 13) covering all three Senate candidates. Covers Kelly's positioning as a candidate with proven electoral track record in the south suburbs.

news_hits.headline: Illinois Senate race yields intense Democratic primary battle
news_hits.title: Illinois Senate race yields intense Democratic primary battle
news_hits.summary: The Hill's March 14 eve-of-primary recap covers the intense three-way Democratic battle for Dick Durbin's seat. Krishnamoorthi, Stratton, and Kelly are blitzing ads and drawing support from national figures as they barrel toward Tuesday's March 17 showdown. Notes early voting turnout could approach 30%, well above typical midterm primaries.

news_hits.headline: Robin Kelly lays out her case in 2026 Illinois Senate primary
news_hits.title: Robin Kelly lays out her case in 2026 Illinois Senate primary
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago profiles Kelly's argument for the Senate seat, noting she is at a fundraising disadvantage ($25M Krishnamoorthi war chest, Pritzker backing for Stratton), but says her message is based on proven effectiveness rather than dollars and advertising.

news_hits.title: On final weekend before Illinois primary, candidates try to keep it local despite flood of out-of-state money
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune's final-weekend dispatch covers candidates canvassing the South Side Irish Parade, Black churches, and local rallies on the eve of the March 17 primary. Preckwinkle, Reilly, Krishnamoorthi, Kelly, and Stratton all named; the piece emphasizes how tens of millions in out-of-state PAC money (pro-Israel AIPAC, crypto, AI interests) have nationalized what were traditionally local races.

news_hits.title: Illinois' Senate Democratic primary a lesson in campaign finance and a test of Gov. JB Pritzker's power
news_hits.summary: Tribune's definitive pre-primary Senate analysis: the contest has narrowed to Krishnamoorthi, Kelly, and Stratton. The race is framed as a test of Gov. Pritzker's political power through his backing of Stratton, his two-term running mate. Notes the cascade of outside PAC money since Durbin's retirement announcement and the role of Trump's second term (immigration raids, Iran war, federal funding threats) as the backdrop.

news_hits.title: Raja Krishnamoorthi, Juliana Stratton and Robin Kelly stick to script in Senate race's final TV debate
news_hits.summary: The three leading Democratic Senate candidates met for their final debate before the March 17 primary, largely sticking to familiar talking points. Krishnamoorthi and Stratton continued clashing over outside donors; Kelly maintained a steadier, lower-conflict posture.

news_hits.title: What to expect in the Illinois state primary
news_hits.summary: AP Decision Notes preview of the March 17 Illinois primary. Senate race: Kelly, Krishnamoorthi, and Stratton (Pritzker-endorsed) top the Democratic field; Krishnamoorthi leads in fundraising with ~$6.6M cash on hand. Pritzker is unopposed for Democratic governor renomination. Republican governor field includes multiple challengers trying to prevent Pritzker's third term.

news_hits.title: 4 things to watch Tuesday in Illinois primaries, including a hotly contested Senate battle
news_hits.summary: Election-eve preview from STLPR covering Illinois primary watchpoints. Senate race (Kelly/Krishnamoorthi/Stratton) is top race; candidates have diverged on minimum wage ($25/hr Stratton vs. $17/hr Kelly and Krishnamoorthi). Race has been contentious over MAGA-aligned and corporate donor accusations between Krishnamoorthi and Stratton.

news_hits.title: Cold weather to greet Illinois voters Tuesday as primary features open US Senate, crowded House races
news_hits.summary: Tribune election-eve dispatch (March 16) confirms Kelly, Krishnamoorthi, and Stratton as the three leading Democratic candidates for the open U.S. Senate seat, with additional minor candidates also listed. Cold weather forecast for primary day. The article notes campaign signs for Pritzker and Stratton at Chicago-area early voting sites — a sign of the governor's active investment in the Senate race and his own third-term bid.

news_hits.title: What to Watch in Tuesday's Illinois Primaries — and Where the Money Went
news_hits.summary: Election Day guide covers the competitive three-way Democratic Senate primary. Kelly, Krishnamoorthi, and Stratton highlighted as the top-tier contenders. A late internal poll shows the race within the margin of error between Krishnamoorthi (31.6%) and Stratton (29.4%), with Kelly at 8%.

news_hits.title: Illinois Election Live Updates: Voting Underway in Senate Primary to Succeed Durbin
news_hits.summary: Two House members Robin Kelly and Raja Krishnamoorthi are competing with Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton for the Democratic nomination to succeed retiring Sen. Dick Durbin.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois primary day election results
news_hits.summary: Illinois primary to succeed retiring Sen. Dick Durbin includes Robin Kelly, Raja Krishnamoorthi, and Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton among the Democrats running.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Map: Live Election Results
news_hits.summary: Robin Kelly, a congresswoman from Chicagoland, has been endorsed by Cory Booker and former Chicago Mayor Lori Lightfoot; offers alternative in a race defined by Krishnamoorthi fundraising vs. Stratton establishment backing.

news_hits.title: Election Night: Kelly trails in Senate primary with 20.3% at 54% counted
news_hits.summary: Rep. Robin Kelly is running third in the Illinois Democratic Senate primary with 20.3% of the vote (138,922 votes) at 54% counted, behind Stratton's 39% and Krishnamoorthi's 32.6%. Kelly is leading in Kankakee County (+7), which falls within her congressional district, but she is trailing badly statewide with the large Cook County vote breaking heavily for Stratton.

news_hits.title: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton wins Democratic nomination for US Senate
news_hits.summary: Stratton wins with ~39.7%, Krishnamoorthi conceded at about 9:30 p.m. Robin Kelly came in third at ~18.4%. In her concession remarks, Kelly bemoaned the big spending that dominates high-profile races, saying it discourages 'good people' from running for or remaining in public office.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton defeats Raja Krishnamoorthi in Senate Democratic primary battle for Dick Durbin's seat
news_hits.summary: Stratton wins the Illinois Democratic Senate primary. Robin Kelly, who ran on a platform opposing outsized donor influence, placed third and conceded Tuesday night. The race was widely seen as a test of progressive messaging on immigration and ICE amid Trump's Operation Midway Blitz.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC's Power Is Tested
news_hits.summary: NYT morning-after analysis of the Illinois primary. Robin Kelly placed a distant third in the Senate race despite running on a strong progressive record. The race was dominated by Stratton's late surge powered by Pritzker money and anti-ICE messaging.

news_hits.title: Progressive Juliana Stratton wins Illinois Democratic Senate primary race
news_hits.summary: The Guardian covers Juliana Stratton's primary victory over Robin Kelly and Raja Krishnamoorthi. Rep. Kelly, who finished third with 18.4% of the vote, was recognized as a strong early contender who was ultimately outspent in the final weeks of the race.

news_hits.title: Robin Kelly concedes Illinois Democratic Senate primary to Juliana Stratton
news_hits.summary: Rep. Robin Kelly conceded in the Democratic race for the Illinois U.S. Senate seat, as Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton won the Democratic primary over Kelly and Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi. Kelly had criticized Gov. Pritzker's involvement in the race, arguing a sitting governor should not interfere.

news_hits.title: 'He has to justify what he did': Black leaders slam JB Pritzker after Illinois primary
news_hits.summary: Breaking March 19 story: CBC Chair Yvette Clarke lashed out at Pritzker, saying she was 'beyond frustrated' with the governor for 'tipping the scales' by funneling $5M from his super PAC to boost Stratton over Krishnamoorthi — the CBC's preferred candidate. Kelly had conceded the night before. The story raises questions about whether the Pritzker-CBC rift will affect party unity heading into the November general election, and about Pritzker's 2028 presidential ambitions.

primary_result.note: Third-place finish. Stratton won with ~39.7%, Krishnamoorthi second with ~33.4%.
